title: Jerjerrod (admiral)
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Youmay|the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]|[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Tiaan Jerjerrod/Legends|Tiaan Jerjerrod]]}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image= |name=Jerjerrod |homeworld=[[Tinnel IV/Legends|Tinnel IV]]{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|9|Welcome On Board}} |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns= |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=*[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]{{WEGCite|story=Admiral Jerjerrod's Testimony|book=Imperial Sourcebook|reprint=1}} **[[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|…every being serving knows that the weapons he uses, the ships on which he serves, the droids and computers on which his life depends—every piece of equipment which he will expect to use—was designed as a result of a Senate compromise to keep each constituent commercial interest happy. Upon entering combat with such equipment a crewman's sole consolation is a grim satisfaction that our enemies probably stole their equipment from us.|Admiral Jerjerrod|}} '''Jerjerrod''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] serving in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Navy]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. Coming from a well-to-do family, Jerjerrod was an ally of Republic [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]; this association provided the admiral's family with many connections. Jerjerrod filed multiple reports with [[Naval Command (Galactic Republic)|Naval Command]] that cited personal concerns with the [[Navy/Legends|Navy's]] procurement procedures of the [[Time/Legends|time]]; when the contents of these documents were leaked to the [[Media/Legends|media]], the admiral was summoned to testify before the [[Naval Subcommittee/Legends|Senate Naval Subcommittee's]] [[Procurement Panel/Legends|Procurement Panel]]. Jerjerrod defended the position before [[Senator/Legends|Senators]] [[Gopple]] and [[V'troren Tara|V'troren]] and denied responsiblity, implying it was another dissatisfied member of the [[Republic Military/Legends|military]], who had revealed the contents of the admiral's reports to the public. ==Biography== {{Quote|Could you explain why you insist on undermining the Republic's confidence in her Navy with your public statements, rather than going through the chain of command as is proper for a military officer?''"
"''The Senator from Erigorm has not scanned my reports very thoroughly. I have reported to the proper people above me and to my peers within the Naval Command. My public statements, as you choose to call them Senator, were taken from those reports I so filed. I had nothing to do with their dissemination.|Senator Gopple and Admiral Jerjerrod|}} Hailing from a wealthy family on the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' of [[Tinnel IV/Legends|Tinnel IV]], Jerjerrod was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as an [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Republic Navy/Legends|Navy]]. An ally of [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], the Republic's [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]], Jerjerrod's family benefited from the connections presented by the alliance with the Chancellor. [[File:Jerjerrod-hd.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Tiaan Jerjerrod, Admiral Jerjerrod's grandson]] Jerjerrod became concerned with the [[Navy/Legends|Navy's]] procurement policies, knowing that every piece of equipment in use—from [[Starship/Legends|starships]] to weapons—was the result of a compromise between the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]] and various commercial interests, and that much of the gear used by the Republic's enemies had likely been stolen from the [[Republic Military/Legends|military]]. The admiral filed several reports regarding the matter with superiors and colleagues within [[Naval Command (Galactic Republic)|Naval Command]]; many of the reports were subsequently leaked to the [[Media/Legends|media]]. Believed to be responsible for the leaks, Jerjerrod was summoned to appear before the [[Procurement Panel/Legends|Procurement Panel of the Senate Naval Subcommittee]]. The admiral was questioned by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Gopple]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]{{TORcite|type=codex|The Rift Alliance (Consular)}} [[Erigorm]] and Senator [[V'troren Tara|V'troren]], both members of the Procurement Panel. Gopple accused Jerjerrod of attempting to weaken the Republic's faith in the Navy, but the admiral maintained that the appropriate measures had been taken in filing the reports, rejecting responsibility for the leak. V'troren was not convinced, believing the admiral to be the only one with the motivation required to release the reports to the media. Jerjerrod responded by hinting that there were many dissatisfied [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] within the military who would wish to have the admiral's reports made public. [[Tiaan Jerjerrod/Legends|Tiaan Jerjerrod]], future [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] and [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], was Jerjerrod's grandson. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|But if you did not leak these reports to the media, who did? Who else would have had the motivation to promulgate your particular point of view?|Senator V'troren|}} Jerjerrod was noted as being fiery and resolute, traits that the admiral's grandson, Tiaan, would not possess. The admiral was particularly concerned with the Republic Navy's procurement policies, which prompted Jerjerrod to write several reports to Naval Command on the subject. When the contents of these documents were leaked to the public, and when the admiral was summoned before the [[Naval Subcommittee/Legends|Naval Subcommittee's]] Procurement Panel, Jerjerrod denied responsibility for the leak, instead suggesting that the admiral's point of view was shared by many members of the military and that any one of them could be responsible for the breach. ==Behind the scenes== The character of Jerjerrod first appeared in "[[Admiral Jerjerrod's Testimony]]," a brief questions-and-answers session between Jerjerrod and two senators written by [[Greg Gorden]] that is presented as a segment of an [[in-universe]] record in the [[1989]] [[Imperial Sourcebook|first edition of the ''Imperial Sourcebook'']].''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]'' ==Appearances== *{{WEGCite|story=Admiral Jerjerrod's Testimony|book=Imperial Sourcebook|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|9|Welcome On Board}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Individuals with unspecified pronouns]] [[Category:Jerjerrod family]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Republic Navy admirals]]
